meta.description: Scaffold and configure a new Remotion project from an episode format template

step.name: determine_template
step.action: Read the episode format from the SSD or episode configuration. Map the format to the appropriate Remotion project template. Available templates may include: standard (16:9 landscape), short-form (9:16 vertical), square (1:1), or custom. If no matching template exists, use the standard template as a base and adjust dimensions. Log the selected template and reasoning.

step.name: scaffold_from_template
step.action: Scaffold a new Remotion project from the selected template into episodes/{episode_id}/remotion/. Copy the template structure including: src/ directory with Root component, package.json with Remotion dependencies, tsconfig.json, and any template-specific components. Ensure the project directory structure follows Remotion conventions. Do not overwrite any existing files if the directory already contains content.

step.name: configure_remotion_config
step.action: Configure remotion.config.ts with episode-specific settings. Set the composition width and height based on the target resolution from the SSD. Set the FPS (frames per second) to match the project specification (typically 30). Set the default codec and encoding settings. Configure the webpack override if custom asset loading is needed. Set the output location for renders.

step.name: set_up_asset_references
step.action: Configure the project to reference episode assets. Set up the public/ directory or staticFile paths to point to the episode asset directories (video clips, audio files, images). Create type-safe asset path helpers if using TypeScript. Verify that asset paths resolve correctly from within the Remotion project. Document the asset directory mapping for other workflows.

step.name: install_dependencies
step.action: Run npm install in the project directory to install all dependencies. Verify Remotion core packages are installed at compatible versions (@remotion/cli, @remotion/player, remotion). Install any additional packages required by the template (e.g., @remotion/media-utils for audio visualization). Resolve any dependency conflicts. Verify package-lock.json is generated.

step.name: verify_preview_starts
step.action: Start the Remotion preview server using npx remotion preview. Verify the development server starts without errors. Confirm the composition is listed and selectable in the Remotion Studio. Verify video and audio assets load correctly in the preview. Check that the timeline displays the correct duration. Stop the preview server after verification. Log any warnings encountered during startup.
step.output: Preview verification report confirming project is ready

quality-gates.gate: Episode format mapped to correct Remotion template
Project scaffolded with complete directory structure
remotion.config.ts configured with correct resolution, FPS, and codec
Asset references resolve correctly from within the project
Remotion preview server starts without errors
Composition listed and playable in Remotion Studio
